name: Date Sandwich Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure dates are at room temperature. Slice them thinly and break up in the pan.
2. Combine dates, water, orange peel, and juices in a saucepan. Blend well. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until thickened, about 10 minutes. Cool.
3. Measure flour into a large bowl. Add remaining ingredients except margarine. Stir well to blend.
4. Cut in margarine until the mixture consists of particles the size of peas.
5. Spread half of the crumb mixture in a greased 8 x 8-inch pan and press down.
6. Spread the cooled date filling evenly over the crumbs.
7. Cover with the remaining crumb mixture, pressing lightly.
8. Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes until golden.
9. Cool in the pan on a rack. Cut into 18 bars.
(Sugartwin version) 1 bar ≈ 1/2 fruit, 1 fat, 97 calories.
From High Fiber Cookbook - a bit crumbly but delicious.
